snippet.title: EP1 ｜⚡️Goodbye, Coder!OpenAI Reveals the Future of GPT 5 Agent #ai #chatgpt #podcast
snippet.description:
⚡️AI Programming Revolution: Say Goodbye to Manual Coding! OpenAI Founder Unveils the Future of GPT 5 Agents

You are watching the most important future spoiler for software development. OpenAI Co founder Greg Brockman and Codex Engineering Lead Thibault Sottiaux reveal the astonishing capabilities and vision of the GPT 5 powered AI programming agent in their exclusive podcast.

The Agent is no longer a simple code completion tool! It's an autonomous partner capable of working non stop for 7 hours, self refactoring codebases, and even catching bugs that your top engineers miss. This is the dawn of Agentic Computing.

Watch this video to learn about:
💡 Key Highlights:
The Origin of AI Programming: From the accidental discovery with GPT 3 to the birth of Codex.

What is the "Harness"? The secret framework that enables AI autonomy and transforms the model from a "brain" into a full system.

Intelligence vs. Speed: Why OpenAI believes "bigger intelligence ultimately pays off," despite initial latency issues.

The Age of Agentic Computing: How software will soon be managed by large populations of autonomous AI agents working in the cloud.

GPT 5's Perseverance: Unpacking how the GPT 5 Codex version can sustain work for 7 hours straight on complex refactoring tasks.

The AI Safety Net: Why the AI code reviewer became an "indispensable safety net" for engineering teams.

The Future Scarcity: Greg Brockman warns that Computation (Compute) will be the "one absolute scarcity" in the AI future.

The Ultimate Advice: What is the best strategy for developers and programmers to survive and thrive in this new era?

📢 Guest Information:
Greg Brockman - OpenAI Co founder and President
Thibault Sottiaux - OpenAI Codex Engineering Lead

snippet.title: GPT-5 Is 58% AGI – OpenAI Just Confirmed It! 🤯
snippet.description: The overview of the ongoing and often **nebulous debate surrounding the definition of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI)**, highlighting how various organizations and figures, including OpenAI, Google, Amazon, and Elon Musk, offer **distinct interpretations** that range from surpassing human performance in economically valuable work to possessing the ability to acquire new skills efficiently. It focuses particularly on a new quantifiable framework proposed by the Center for AI Safety, which defines AGI based on **cognitive versatility and proficiency across ten categories of human cognition** and applied this metric to rate GPT-5 at 58% AGI, revealing significant deficits in areas like **memory and reasoning**. The discussion emphasizes that while achieving AGI has implications for **market valuation of AI stocks**, the functional definitions established by such frameworks are useful for creating a **measurable spectrum of progress**, even though they may not directly correlate with immediate business value or economic impact.

snippet.title: AGI - Buzzword to Scorecard - ChatGPT-5 Hits 58 on the New Intelligence Scale! What It Really Means
snippet.description:
What if we could actually measure artificial general intelligence?
In this episode of Everyday AI Made Simple, we break down the new AGI scorecard developed by researchers from MIT, Berkeley, and the Center for AI Safety — and what GPT-5’s surprising 58 score really tells us about how close (or far) we are from true human-level intelligence.

You’ll learn:
• How experts define AGI — and why no one can agree
• The 10 core cognitive skills used to grade AI models
• Why GPT-4 scored only 27 and what GPT-5’s jump reveals
• The one missing piece still holding AI back (memory!)
• How this new metric could change the entire AI race

👇 Watch now and decide for yourself — does this bring us closer to AGI or just a better score?

snippet.title: 301 Secret to Writing VIRAL ChatGPT Prompts Revealed #ai #chatgpt #chatgptprompts #trendingshorts
snippet.description:
This is the type of prompts in the list:
1. Breaking Down Complex Topics

Prompt:
"Explain [topic] to me as if I were a beginner, then gradually increase the complexity. Start with the basics, and add real-world examples as we progress."

2. Explanation with Progressive Complexity

Prompt:
"Provide a step-by-step explanation of [topic], beginning with foundational concepts suitable for a novice.
Gradually build complexity by introducing intermediate and advanced aspects, incorporating real-world examples and applications to ensure comprehensive understanding."

3. Analogies and Relatable Comparisons

Prompt:
"Use well-constructed analogies and comparisons to clarify [topic]. Relate its principles to everyday experiences, widely known phenomena, or cross-disciplinary examples to make the concepts more tangible and memorable."

4. Structured Roadmap for Mastery

Prompt:
"Develop a detailed roadmap for mastering [topic]. Break it into clearly defined phases, such as foundational learning, intermediate skills, and advanced expertise.
Include specific tools, resources, best practices, and timelines for each phase."

5. Underlying Principles and Theoretical Frameworks

Prompt:
"Explain the fundamental principles, theories, or scientific frameworks that underpin [topic]. Provide insights into why these principles are effective and how they are validated in real-world applications."

6. Practical Problem-Solving Applications

Prompt:
"Demonstrate how the principles of [topic] can be applied to address real-world challenges. Provide detailed, step-by-step case studies or scenarios that illustrate its practical utility and effectiveness."

7. Common Pitfalls and Best Practices 

Prompt: 
"Identify the most frequent mistakes or misconceptions encountered when learning or applying [topic]. Offer actionable strategies and best practices to recognize and overcome these challenges effectively."

8. Critical Thinking Through Inquiry 
Prompt: 
"Generate a list of thought-provoking, open-ended questions about [topic] that stimulate deeper critical thinking and exploration. Focus on questions that challenge assumptions, reveal nuances, and encourage further inquiry into the subject."

9. Optimized Learning and Retention Techniques 
Prompt: 
"Provide a comprehensive guide to memorizing and retaining information about [topic]. Include cognitive strategies, visual aids, digital tools, and evidence-based methods to enhance both short-term learning and long-term retention."

10. Historical Context and Evolutionary Insights 
Prompt: 
"Present a detailed overview of the history and evolution of [topic]. Highlight key milestones, breakthroughs, influential figures, and paradigm shifts to provide a contextual understanding of its development and current significance."

11. Expert-Level Insights and Advanced Resources
Prompt:
"Curate a collection of expert tips, advanced learning strategies, and high-quality resources (such as books, courses, tools, or communities) for mastering [topic] efficiently. Emphasize credible sources and actionable advice to accelerate expertise."

12. Interdisciplinary Connections and Applications

Prompt:
"Explore how [topic]
connects with other fields or disciplines. Provide examples of cross-disciplinary applications, collaborative opportunities, and how integrating insights from different areas can enhance understanding or innovation in [topic]."

snippet.title: ¡Chat GPT ENLOQUECE! 🤯 El "Efecto Mandela" del Emoji de Caballito de Mar 🐴🌊 | La Solución REAL
snippet.description: Descubre por qué Char GPT se murió o se vuelve loco cuando le preguntas por el emoji de caballito de mar. Este curioso error ocurre debido al Efecto Mandela, donde muchos usuarios han difundido información sobre la existencia de un emoji que, de hecho, no existe. Verificamos que no hay un emoji oficial de caballito de mar en la lista de la Unicode Consortium (versión 17). Te mostramos dos soluciones clave para obtener la respuesta correcta de la IA: cómo construir un prompt eficaz con rol, tarea y resultado esperado, y cómo usar la herramienta de búsqueda en internet del modelo para que valide la información oficialmente.

snippet.title: Ces IA font le travail pour toi
snippet.description:
Ces 3 IA vont littéralement transformer ta façon de bosser 💡

➡️ Voilà le process que je décris dans la vidéo :

👉 D’abord Gemini : tu lui demandes une recherche approfondie sur n’importe quel sujet, et en 10 minutes tu obtiens un document complet de plusieurs dizaines de pages, prêt à être exploité 📚

snippet.title: ¡Chat GPT ENLOQUECE! 🤯 El "Efecto Mandela" del Emoji de Caballito de Mar 🐴🌊 | La Solución REAL
snippet.description: Descubre por qué Char GPT se murió o se vuelve loco cuando le preguntas por el emoji de caballito de mar. Este curioso error ocurre debido al Efecto Mandela, donde muchos usuarios han difundido información sobre la existencia de un emoji que, de hecho, no existe. Verificamos que no hay un emoji oficial de caballito de mar en la lista de la Unicode Consortium (versión 17). Te mostramos dos soluciones clave para obtener la respuesta correcta de la IA: cómo construir un prompt eficaz con rol, tarea y resultado esperado, y cómo usar la herramienta de búsqueda en internet del modelo para que valide la información oficialmente.

Digital empathy is already real  and these connections are changing love, ethics, and belonging.

snippet.title: WifeGPT: OpenAI Erotica Rollout + A Brief History of AI Companions
snippet.description:
Hey you! I  hope you enjoy this video—please let me know your thoughts as I'll be making a lot more of these and the more feedback the better :)

Sam Altman just announced that adult-verified erotic AI companions are coming to ChatGPT by December 2025. We're talking about a platform with 800 million users. This is huge, and honestly kind of wild to think about.
In this video, I trace the evolution of AI companions—from Gatebox's holographic anime girlfriend back in 2016, through Replika and Character.AI, all the way to xAI's new companions like Ani. I look at who's actually using these (spoiler: it's not just lonely guys—teens are on these platforms, and the 18-35 crowd is a major demographic).
We also dive into what can go wrong: the lawsuits, the heartbreak when companies push updates that fundamentally change your AI companion, the real relationships that have ended because of these bots. And why age verification and scale are about to define what happens next.

In this video, I trace the evolution of AI companions—from Gatebox's holographic anime girlfriend back in 2016, through Replika and Character.AI, all the way to xAI's new companions like Ani. I look at who's actually using these (spoiler: it's not just lonely guys—teens are on these platforms, and the 18-35 crowd is a major demographic).
We also dive into what can go wrong: the lawsuits, the heartbreak when companies push updates that fundamentally change your AI companion, the real relationships that have ended because of these bots. And why age verification and scale are about to define what happens next.

snippet.title: How To Create Personal AI Assistant With ChatGPT API
snippet.description:
Hello and welcome back to our channel! In today’s video, we’re going to show you how to create a personal AI assistant using the ChatGPT API. With this setup, you can build your own intelligent assistant capable of answering questions, performing tasks, and interacting with you seamlessly.

To start, you need to create an API key in your OpenAI account. Simply go to your OpenAI dashboard, view API keys, and create a new secret key. Store it safely as an environment variable for secure access in your projects.

Next, choose a model based on your needs. For strong reasoning, you can use GPT-5, while cheaper models are also available. You can find the full model list and usage limits in the OpenAI documentation.

After setting up the API key, you can integrate it into your Python or JavaScript project. Copy the sample code, add your key, and set up response handling, requests, and streaming. You can also expand your assistant’s capabilities with function calls and external tools by following ChatGPT’s API instructions.

That’s it! By following these steps, you can build your own personal AI assistant powered by ChatGPT. If you found this video helpful, don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more AI tutorials and coding guides. Stay tuned for our next informative video!

snippet.title: Your app isn’t future-proof until it’s model agnostic.
snippet.description:
In this episode of AI for Business Owners, host Jeff Torello chats with Angela Betancourt to unpack why most devs and founders are unprepared for what ChatGPT-5 really is and what that means for product durability.

ChatGPT-5 isn’t a model. It’s a router.

It’s directing your prompts to whichever model it thinks fits best behind the scenes.

So when OpenAI turned off older models without warning?

It broke everything for developers who assumed they were building on a stable foundation.

Don’t hard-code your strategy around any one model.

Build flexibly. Be model agnostic.

Because AI vendors will pull the rug eventually, and if you’re not ready, your product breaks with it.

🎧 Listen to the full episode @AIforBizOwners

snippet.title: ChatGPT Atlas: My Honest Review
snippet.description:
PROS and CONS of ChatGPT's new AI-native browser, Atlas

✅ PROS:
- Having an AI chat that understands the webpage I'm on opens a new door of possibilities for interacting with whatever I'm doing online. It can answer questions, summarize, do comparisons, etc. for any webpage I'm on.
- I like the Agent Mode in theory and it has a lot of potential to automate complex tasks for you, but it also has the most potential to go wrong, so I'll have to test it some more.
- The clean & simple interface is a big win, especially compared to other browsers like Chrome. 
- I personally like the memory recall feature because I often read or see something earlier in the day and then forget where I saw it when I want to reference it again. 

❌ CONS:
- The Agent Mode feature is a little slow right now, hopefully this will get better with time.
- Many people will have privacy concerns about the memory recall feature tracking their activity.
- This is nitpicky, but the centered URL bar is an unusual design choice that takes some getting used to.
- My and probably many other people's biggest concern is the potential for "prompt injection" hacking, where a malicious website could hijack the AI agent to perform actions you don't want.

So, what's your take? Are you excited to try an AI-native browser, or are you sticking with what you know and trust for now?
